item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: he image that was used in this article could not be found under any source as cc-compliant. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for the necropost, but I agree with this very strongly. This is a prime example of blackboxing done exactly right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More convenience. I'm not good enough at Photoshop to make a picture of a brand brand myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
image is of a mercedes brand, which means that its not a D-classes arm.. why would a D-Class be giving mercedes money?

researcher was clumsy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I'm sorry to hear you weren't sold. For what it's worth, George wasn't mad, he was just unfortunate. He tried to make something that would raise people's awareness, and accidentally took it too far. He just wanted stamps, tattoos, something like that - certainly not burns. And he definitely didn't think it would kill him…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just because it's not AWCY doesn't make the seemingly ever present mad artist trope any more original.

It's a cursed item - okay… I wasn't sold by the hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could remember.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hurm, out of interest, to what other skip are you referring?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to popular demand, I'll remove the footnotes.

Also, as well as the pun in the name very deliberately not in the name, there's another tiny Easter egg in the meta information…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same. I also feel refuting his inaccuracies is overkill when his burnt body is a more subtle demonstration that he didn't fully get what he was talking about.

I love the idea though, and that picture is frightening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yet another victim to the SDF group of interest.

(some dumb fool)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel dumb that I didn't get the double entendre until Scantron's comment. I claim willful ignorance.

I do definitely like that he was killed by the very thing he was against. And I want to point out that this comment:

As for George… he's not a genius or insane, no. Like almost everyone. He's just a guy that had an idea which worked out a lot better than he expected.

sounds an awful lot like commentary on another skip. That's a very effective path to take.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I too think that the footnotes don't fit, other than that, I really enjoyed this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, but did the footnotes seem strange to anyone else? It felt like the in-universe author was editorializing. Tone just seemed kind of off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did initially consider having the Foundation be on there and be mysteriously owning companies, but it wouldn't really make sense from a business perspective. The vast majority of such large companies are publicly traded and, consequently, have a certain amount of their information available to the general public. Unless the Foundation actually is, say, Unilever, and somehow is allowed to not reveal its real status, it wouldn't make sense for it to own stakes but not be reflected in records. People would ask too many questions.

As for George… he's not a genius or insane, no. Like almost everyone. He's just a guy that had an idea which worked out a lot better than he expected.

Also you don't know for sure it was him that made it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did upvote this but personally I think the final addendum is a bit weak. I mean, he doesn't really sound like a complete crackpot and he also doesn't really sound like a genius. I would have preferred it if he was shown to be completely crazy (lizards are all millionaires) or possibly shown to know things that The Foundation thought no one else knew (X organisation ((perhaps the Foundation)) owns Y). Otherwise it seems he has no biznizz making anomalous objects

Also it would be pretty funny to think that The Foundation are all worried about people going out and buying KY Jelly and getting a massive Foundation logo emblazoned on their forehead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Far more clever than a simple exploitation of the double meaning of "brand" has any right to be, and very easily believable. I also like the detail of the blackboxed name — who are they? Why does the Foundation feel the need to remove the name? How did Whitby find out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad. The effects of looking at the painting are pretty clever. And thank you for not immediately pigeonholing this as an AWCY item.

I'm sticking a little bit on the way this is bludgeoning the reader, though. In context of the article, I don't see why the Foundation would bother to refute the guy's claims, it doesn't seem relevant at all to containing the object.

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a cool picture. I notice that both PepsiCo and Unilever own Lipton, and that's my only observation on the thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nooo! Capitalism is bad, this guy just chose a poor way to express his dislike of it, and then it got even more out of hand than he'd anticipated…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes indeed! That's basically the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Love the idea. Love the length. Love that it's an art piece but not AWCY, love the guy killed by his own righteous ignorance, love the wrong facts.

Go Capitalism!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. And yet no one owns Valve…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bung it in a collapsible with a "LEVEL SKREEDLY-DEEDLY ACCESS REQUIRED" on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It also reminds me of the image I've seen floating around the past couple of years that shows how a few parent companies produce nearly all the major brands we know.

http://alexthecopywriter.files.wordpress.com/2013/04/155b2e11_parent-companies-large.jpg

Was that part of the inspiration?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeesh… the Valve logo alone would kill me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: and yet poor George executes it so terribly…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great concept. It reminds me of the idea that I've seen in several places that congressmen should have to wear suits with the logos and names of everyone who donated to their campaign, sort of like NASCAR.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is acceptable.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… there is one incredibly important word I accidentally omitted.

Edit: I have now put it in (it was 'partial') and also made it more clear in other places.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is indeed a good piece. Not too short and not too long, with just enough information to close me out satisfied. That it's an Anart piece that has nothing to do with AWCY is also something I like.

One thing I do wonder about though… Since this SCP-1854-2 is basically an infohazard triggered by SCP-1854-1, I'm dubious as to whether or not there's a good reason in-universe to include Addendum 1854-B in the document. This is going to be given to all personnel who are assigned to work on the object. So… after having read this document, none of those people can ever, ever look at the painting they're containing. Having seen the painting itself, one can never ever read this SCP document in its entirety.

The potential for accidental exposure is too high. I have to no-vote until you come up with a way to resolve this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, there can be any length of time between the two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The anomalous effect of SCP-1854 occurs when SCP-1854-1 is viewed and SCP-1854-2 is comprehended. Any length of time may pass between these two events without affecting the immediate trigger time of SCP-1854.

Does this mean you need to ehar it and see it at the same time to activate the skip? Just to be more clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what the community is for!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially blackboxed it, but on later thought I realised I couldn't think of any good reason for the Foundation to censor it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good work! This is much improved, I'm glad you took the time to work on it. <3 and upvotes for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Killed by his own righteous indignation. Nice. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's more of an author's prerogative, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely an interesting one, but isn't it usual for the name(s) of any creator(s) to be blackboxed unless it has relevance to the piece?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
New SCP. This came about for a couple of reasons - the pun, and the frustration I feel at people with whose sentiments I agree but whose methods seem counterproductive (e.g. I remember a group of protesters who managed to get a politician out of his car and looking at their petition by being reasonable and polite, but then someone at the back started hurling abuse and threw something - so of course the politician left and denounced the people for their violence. The petition was ignored).

Thanks to:

- Roget
- Dmatix
- Cerxi (who supplied the image)
- Sciurus
- tahunu
- Randomini

who provided criticism which helped me refine this.
